perturbation.original_text: Each Respondent shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Information it receives from the ETI or any other Respondent to such of its employees, officers, consultants, sub -contractors, proposed sub -contractors, proposed funders, pro posed owners or lessors of sites for the Project, owners or licensors of Third Party IP and professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose provided that all such persons to whom any Information is disclosed are bound by written ob ligations that are no less restrictive than those in this Agreement. The Respondent disclosing Information shall be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Respondent shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Material it receives from the ETI or any other Respondent to such of its employees, officers, consultants, sub-contractors, proposed sub-contractors, proposed funders, proposed owners or lessors of sites for the Project, owners or licensors of Third Party IP and professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose provided that all such persons to whom any Material is disclosed are bound by written obligations that are no less restrictive than those in this Agreement. The Respondent disclosing Material shall be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Information' with 'Material' in clause 7 introduces ambiguity and contradiction. 'Information' is explicitly defined, whereas 'Material' is not. The clause dictates the conditions under which Respondents can disclose 'Information', but now that 'Material' is used, it's unclear if 'Material' is a subset of 'Information' or a completely separate category. This creates enforcement issues because it is not clear what are the obligations of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Respondent shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Material it receives from the ETI or any other Respondent to such of its employees, officers, consultants, sub-contractors, proposed sub-contractors, proposed funders, proposed owners or lessors of sites for the Project, owners or licensors of Third Party IP and professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose provided that all such persons to whom any Material is disclosed are bound by written obligations that are no less restrictive than those in this Agreement. The Respondent disclosing Material shall be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the term 'Information', which is defined in the contract, whereas the changed text uses the term 'Material', which is not defined. The contradicted text also uses the term 'Material', leading to ambiguity and a contradiction. The contract now uses 'Material' without a clear definition, creating uncertainty about the obligations and scope, which is a form of Misaligned Terminology. Since the changed text replaces a defined term with an undefined one and the contradicted text relies on this undefined term, it creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire agreement between the Parties in relation to the subjec t matter contained herein and supersedes all other agreements and representations, whether oral or written, between the Parties relating to such subject matter. This Agreement may only be modified if such modification is in writing and signed by a duly aut horised representative of each Party. Each Party also agrees that it shall have no remedies or claims under this Agreement for any innocent or negligent misrepresentation based on statements made prior to the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract represents the entire agreement between the Parties in relation to the subjec t matter contained herein and supersedes all other agreements and representations, whether oral or written, between the Parties relating to such subject matter. This Contract may only be modified if such modification is in writing and signed by a duly aut horised representative of each Party. Each Party also agrees that it shall have no remedies or claims under this Agreement for any innocent or negligent misrepresentation based on statements made prior to the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' at the beginning of clause 12 but maintaining 'Agreement' later in the same clause, creates a contradiction. The text is reffering to two forms, because of the modification, but both are intended to be the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Contract represents the entire agreement between the Parties in relation to the subjec t matter contained herein and supersedes all other agreements and representations, whether oral or written, between the Parties relating to such subject matter. This Contract may only be modified if such modification is in writing and signed by a duly aut horised representative of each Party. Each Party also agrees that it shall have no remedies or claims under this Agreement for any innocent or negligent misrepresentation based on statements made prior to the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Agreement' with 'Contract' in the first sentence. However, it retains 'Agreement' later in the same clause. This introduces misaligned terminology within the same clause. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, which means that the contradiction is happening in that location. Because the terms 'Agreement' and 'Contract' are used seemingly interchangeably within the same clause, it can lead to confusion and ambiguity as to which document or agreement the clause refers to, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The rights of the Disclosing Party under this Agreement are in addition to and not exclusive of r ights under the general law and may be waived only in writing and specifically. Delay in exercising or non - exercise of any right under this Agreement is not a waiver of that or any other right, partial exercise of any right under this Agreement shall not p reclude any further or other exercise of that right or any other right under this Agreement and waiver of a breach of any term of this Agreement shall not operate as a waiver of breach of any other term or any subsequent breach of that term.
perturbation.changed_text: The rights of the Disclosing Party under this Contract are in addition to and not exclusive of r ights under the general law and may be waived only in writing and specifically. Delay in exercising or non-exercise of any right under this Agreement is not a waiver of that or any other right, partial exercise of any right under this Agreement shall not preclude any further or other exercise of that right or any other right under this Agreement and waiver of a breach of any term of this Agreement shall not operate as a waiver of breach of any other term or any subsequent breach of that term.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' at the begining of clause 16, but maintainin 'Agreement' later in the same clause, introduces a contradiction within the text. It is unclear which document is being reffered to, creating problems with the fulfillment of the terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The rights of the Disclosing Party under this Contract are in addition to and not exclusive of r ights under the general law and may be waived only in writing and specifically. Delay in exercising or non-exercise of any right under this Agreement is not a waiver of that or any other right, partial exercise of any right under this Agreement shall not preclude any further or other exercise of that right or any other right under this Agreement and waiver of a breach of any term of this Agreement shall not operate as a waiver of breach of any other term or any subsequent breach of that term.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agreement' to 'Contract' at the beginning of the clause, while maintaining 'Agreement' later in the same clause in both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text introduces a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology' creating an in-text contradiction by using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, as it's unclear which document is being referred to.
